task.title: How to Unclog a Sink Using a Plunger?
task.shortdesc: No cost method that requires a common household item to fix a clogged sink.

taskbody.context:
This is a method that uses the household item, plunger, to create pressure and suction in the drain pipe to help loosen any debris stuck inside. Follow the guide to learn how to use this method.
Materials Needed:PlungerPot / Kettle of Hot Water

step.cmd: After following the guide on how to unplug the drain stopper, the first step you will have to do is to screw the pivot rod back into the drain pipe. Make sure that everything is tight.
Turn on the faucet and check back down at the pipes to see if there are any leaks. If there are any, tighten the nut to prevent water spilling out.
After making sure there are no leaks, fill the sink up just enough to cover the plunger cup.
Take your plunger and align it where the drainage hole is. Start pressing the plunger in the sink to create some suction.
Keep the motion going until you can see the water starting to drain itself.

taskbody.result: Your sink should be able to drain normally now and is back into working condition with no more debris and grime.
